steps.title: Focus on public rankings
steps.content: The planner is making advertisements for the general public, so the relevant data are the general public's rankings. A lower ranking number means the group perceived a stronger benefit. To support the medicinal-plant focus, the evidence must show that the public ranked medicinal plants above the other listed services.

steps.title: Support the medicinal focus
steps.content: Choice A would undermine the planner's conclusion by making soil erosion rank higher. Choice C compares stakeholders with the public, which does not show what the public values most. Choice D is about pollination and project leaders. The needed data show medicinal plants ranked highest among the listed benefits for the general public. The answer is B.
